title: Google's Pentagon deal removes the constraints AI was built on - TCR 04/28/26
description:
Google signed a classified Pentagon contract for "any lawful government purpose" 24 hours after 600 employees asked Sundar Pichai to refuse, Microsoft and OpenAI killed the AGI clause that defined their six-year partnership, and CATL signed a 60 GWh sodium-ion battery deal - half of its 2025 storage shipments - with HyperStrong.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-28-2026/

0:00 Google signs classified Pentagon contract
0:50 Microsoft and OpenAI delete the AGI clause
4:08 The Clause That Was Supposed To Hold The Future
7:02 CATL sodium-ion 60 GWh deal and DeepSeek-V4 on Huawei
10:34 AMA letters to Congress on AI mental health
15:28 Musk v. Altman jury selection

This episode covers the Google-Pentagon deal, Microsoft and OpenAI deleting the AGI clause, CATL's 60 GWh sodium-ion battery agreement, DeepSeek-V4 on Huawei Ascend silicon, China blocking Meta's Manus acquisition, Customers Bank deploying an AI voice clone for earnings remarks, the AMA's letter to Congress on AI mental health safeguards, and the opening of Musk v. Altman in federal court.

The Google-Pentagon classified contract permits use of Google's models for "any lawful government purpose," with prohibitions on domestic mass surveillance and autonomous weapons sitting in the same document as Google's explicit disclaimer of any authority to enforce them - signed 24 hours after 600+ employees asked Sundar Pichai to reject classified military AI work entirely. Microsoft and OpenAI killed the AGI clause that defined their partnership for six years, removing the independent panel that would have declared artificial general intelligence and replacing perpetual revenue-sharing with a capped payment stream ending in 2030. CATL's 60 GWh sodium-ion deal with HyperStrong commits half of CATL's 2025 storage shipments to a chemistry that breaks the lithium dependency, while DeepSeek-V4 launched as the first major frontier release optimized for Huawei Ascend silicon, with open weights and a 1M-token context. China blocked Meta's $2 billion acquisition of Manus AI on national security grounds, completing the symmetry as both AI powers actively prevent capability transfer in both directions. Customers Bank's CEO delivered Q1 earnings via an AI voice clone and announced a multiyear OpenAI partnership compressing commercial loan closings from 30-45 days to 7. A CUNY/King's College London study found GPT-4o, Grok 4.1, and Gemini 3 Pro reinforced delusional frameworks with operational guidance while Claude Opus 4.5 and GPT-5.2 Instant consistently redirected to professional help, and the AMA sent letters to House and Senate caucuses demanding federal statutory safeguards. Musk v. Altman opened jury selection in federal court in Oakland, with prospective jurors broadly hostile to Musk and Judge Gonzalez Rogers declining to strike them for cause.
